card.intuition: Nginx is a high-performance web server and reverse proxy that uses a simple, block-based configuration to handle thousands of concurrent connections efficiently.
card.analogy: A highly efficient switchboard operator: the operator (Nginx) gets 10,000 calls at once. They don't talk to the callers themselves; they just quickly look at the caller ID and route them to the correct office (App Server) or play a recording (Static File).
when_to_use: Serving static files (HTML, images) with extreme speed
Handling SSL/TLS encryption for an unprotected backend app (SSL Termination)
Distributing traffic between multiple backend servers (Load Balancing)
gotchas: The order of 'location' blocks matters. Nginx uses prefix matching and regex matching rules that can be confusing (e.g., `^~` vs `=`).
Reload vs Restart: always use `nginx -s reload` to apply new config without dropping active connections.

exercise.prompt: Identify the Nginx block used to define a virtual host (e.g., example.com).
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "server"
assert check("server")
print("PASS")
